idea-plugin.name: Copilot Monitor
idea-plugin.vendor: Ayrton Rafael Risco Torres (with the help of Claude)
idea-plugin.description:
Copilot Monitor

Real-time visibility into your GitHub Copilot AI usage — directly inside IntelliJ.

  • Context Window — live utilization bar with tab-by-tab breakdown and warnings before you hit limits
  • Token & Cost Tracking — daily/monthly totals, per-model breakdown, budget gauge with 75/90/100% alerts
  • LLM Cache Analysis — cache hit rate trend, savings estimate, and actionable recommendations
  • Performance Metrics — TTFT histogram, latency by model, error rate, finish-reason distribution
  • Productivity Score — acceptance rate, fluency score, feature-usage breakdown
  • Model Comparison — side-by-side context window vs. Copilot limit, cost/1K, cache support
  • OpenTelemetry Export (opt-in) — send spans to any OTLP backend (Grafana, Jaeger, Datadog…)

Privacy-first: all data stays local (SQLite). No code, prompts, or file names are ever stored or logged. No network calls unless you opt in.

Source: github.com/ayrtonrisco/copilot-monitor-plugin
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.1.0 — Bug Fixes
  • Fix: token counter no longer inflates from Copilot auth/telemetry requests — only real AI completions are counted
  • Fix: all dashboard panels (Context, Tokens, Cache, Performance, Models) now display data correctly
  • Fix: daily/weekly charts populate immediately after the first interaction
  • Fix: plugin can be enabled/disabled without IDE restart (dynamic plugin support)
  • Fix: removed internal API usage (AppLifecycleListener → postStartupActivity)
  • Fix: duplicate interactions no longer inserted when Copilot log files are re-read
1.0.0 — Initial Release
  • Context window utilization monitor with live tab breakdown
  • Token consumption tracking (input, output, cache) across all Copilot features
  • Cost estimation against configurable monthly budget with 75/90/100% alerts
  • LLM cache hit rate analysis and savings estimation
  • Performance metrics: TTFT histogram, latency by model, error rate
  • Productivity score and acceptance rate tracking
  • Model comparison table with context window gap visualization
  • OpenTelemetry export (opt-in OTLP HTTP/gRPC)
  • Status bar widget with color-coded token usage
  • Dashboard tool window with 7 panels (Chart.js via JCEF)
  • All data stored locally in SQLite; zero telemetry by default
